food_subgroup: Fermented rice and lentil crepe with spice powder

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general dosa entry (FOODON_00002681). Podi Dosai is a specific variant with milagai podi spice powder; closest_parent used as no exact podi dosai entry exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers dosa as a category. No specific podi dosai sub-code exists; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for dosa or podi dosai in the clinical terminology.

allergens: sesame (present in some milagai podi formulations)

serving.natural_language_portion: One podi dosai weighs approximately 120 g, representing a single crepe as typically served in a South Indian restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Dosa (plain) — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, adapted for podi dosai with milagai podi addition
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base dosa values sourced from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition). Milagai podi (chilli-lentil spice powder) is applied at approximately 10–15 g per 100 g of finished dosai, contributing additional protein, fat, fibre, and sodium. Nutrient values adjusted proportionally based on ICMR-NIN composition data for milagai podi ingredients (roasted chana dal, urad dal, dried red chilli, sesame, salt). Energy, protein, fat, and carbohydrate values reflect the composite dish.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of South Indian fermented foods: dosa and idli — analogous estimate based on plain dosa GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for podi dosai specifically. GI of 55 is based on plain dosa values reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and corroborated by Shobana et al. (2007, European Journal of Clinical Nutrition) which reported GI values of 50–60 for fermented rice-lentil dosa. The addition of milagai podi (dry spice powder with lentils and chilli) does not substantially alter the glycemic response of the base dosa. GL per serving calculated as: (55 × 29.5 × 1.20) / 100 = 19.6 g, using 120 g serving size.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from milagai podi spice powder and added salt
hypertension.guidance: Podi dosai contains approximately 310 mg sodium per 100 g (372 mg per standard 120 g serving), primarily from the milagai podi spice blend and cooking salt; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and request reduced-salt preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 55) and significant carbohydrate load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 55 and a glycemic load of approximately 19.6 per serving, podi dosai has a moderate glycemic impact; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume one dosai as part of a balanced meal with non-starchy vegetables and monitor postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat content and absence of cholesterol in the base preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Podi dosai prepared with minimal oil or ghee provides a relatively low saturated fat profile (approximately 1.4 g per 100 g) and no cholesterol, making it a heart-friendly choice when ghee is limited; however, excess ghee or oil during cooking increases saturated fat intake and should be moderated.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Podi Dosai is a popular South Indian fermented crepe made from a batter of ground rice and urad dal (black gram lentil) that is spread thin on a hot griddle and cooked until crisp. What distinguishes podi dosai from a plain dosa is the generous coating of milagai podi — a dry, coarsely ground spice powder made from roasted lentils (chana dal, urad dal), dried red chillies, sesame seeds, curry leaves, and salt — which is mixed with oil or ghee and applied to the surface of the dosai during cooking. The result is a crispy, slightly spicy, nutty-flavoured crepe with a golden-brown exterior and a fragrant, earthy aroma from the toasted spices. Podi dosai is a staple breakfast and tiffin item across Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, and Andhra Pradesh in South India, and is widely served in South Indian restaurants in Singapore. Nutritionally, podi dosai provides moderate energy (approximately 198 kcal per 100 g), meaningful protein from the lentil-based batter and spice powder, and dietary fibre. The sodium content is elevated due to the spice powder and should be noted by individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low-moderate), making portion control relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is naturally gluten-free and vegetarian, though it may contain sesame allergens from the milagai podi.
common_user_queries: How is podi dosa different from plain dosa?
Is podi dosa good for diabetics?
How many calories in podi dosai?
What spices are in podi dosa?
Where can I get podi dosai in Singapore?

confidence_reason: No exact podi dosai entry exists in ICMR-NIN or HPB Singapore Foodo databases. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN plain dosa data with proportional adjustment for milagai podi contribution. GI is an analogous estimate based on plain dosa GI from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is medium rather than low because the adaptation methodology is transparent and grounded in approved primary sources.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN plain dosa composition with estimated milagai podi contribution; an exact podi dosai entry was not found in ICMR-NIN or HPB Singapore Foodo as of the accessed date. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN dosa entry and milagai podi composition data does not include RAE values. 3. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this dish. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source; dosa is not a significant source of vitamin D. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this dish. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this dish. 7. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as dosa prepared with vegetable oil contains negligible trans fat; confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN and USDA data for similar preparations. 8. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based fermented food with no animal-derived ingredients in the base preparation; naturally zero. 9. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as the base preparation uses plant-based ingredients; naturally zero when prepared without ghee or egg. 10. GI set to 55 based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database plain dosa data and Shobana et al. (2007); gi_category is low per the ≤55 threshold rule. 11. GL per serving = (55 × 29.5 × 1.20) / 100 = 19.6. 12. is_vegan set to false as podi dosai is commonly prepared with ghee; is_dairy_free set to false for the same reason. 13. Sesame allergen note: milagai podi formulations commonly include sesame seeds.

Confirmed all Tamil and Hindi script strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases. Confirmed zero-value justifications for trans_fat_g, vitamin_b12_mcg, and cholesterol_mg in notes. Final decision: PASS.
